unitTitle: Unit 1: Present simple, continuous & stative verbs

content.subtitle: Examples
items: Tom **catches** the bus to school every morning.
My aunt **lives** in a small town by the sea.

content.subtitle: Useful notes
content.description: These time expressions are commonly found with the present simple:
Frequency adverbs normally come before the main verb, but they come after the verb **be**.
items: I **usually walk** to school in the morning.
She is **always** the first to arrive.

content.subtitle: Be careful
notes: The verbs **be** and **have** have irregular present forms.

rows.label: negative
rows.right: He/she/it **is not (isn't / 's not) playing** ... You/we/they **are not (aren't / 're not) playing** ...

content.subtitle: Use
items: Things happening at this very moment
Situations that are temporary and will change

content.subtitle: Examples
items: Sam **is talking** on the phone right now.
Lucy **is staying** with her grandparents this summer.
My sister **is always leaving** her clothes on the floor!

content.subtitle: Useful notes
content.description: These time expressions signal that the present continuous is being used:
**Always** with the present continuous expresses annoyance at something someone keeps doing.
items: Dad **is always forgetting** where he puts his glasses!

content.description: Stative verbs express states — feelings, opinions, senses — rather than actions. Because they describe a condition rather than something happening, we do not normally use them in continuous forms.

content.subtitle: Useful notes
content.description: Stative verbs belong to categories describing inner or permanent conditions, so simple tenses are used.
items: Emotions and preferences: **love, hate, prefer** — She **prefers** tea to coffee.
Mental processes: **know, believe, understand** — He **believes** every word you say.
Ownership: **have, belong to** — This jacket **belongs to** my cousin.
Senses and appearance: **seem, appear** — The idea **seems** a bit unusual.

content.subtitle: Be careful
notes: A small number of stative verbs — including **be**, **have** and **think** — can appear in continuous tenses when they refer to an action rather than a state.
What do you **think** of her new haircut? (= opinion — stative)
**I'm thinking** of changing jobs. (= actively considering — action)

sections.title: A: Present simple from picture prompts
sections.instructions: Look at the prompts and write sentences. Use the correct present simple form.

items.sentence: ...... (every day / get up / at half past seven)
items.answer: Every day, Helen gets up at half past seven.

items.sentence: ...... (often / eat fast food for lunch)
items.answer: She often eats fast food for lunch.

items.sentence: ...... (in the evening / usually / meet her friends for coffee)
items.answer: In the evening, she usually meets her friends for coffee.

items.sentence: ...... (once a week / watch a film at the cinema)
items.answer: She watches a film at the cinema once a week.

items.sentence: ...... (rarely / go to the gym)
items.answer: She rarely goes to the gym.

items.sentence: ...... (have a driving lesson / twice a week)
items.answer: She has a driving lesson twice a week.

sections.title: B: Present continuous
sections.instructions: Complete using the correct present continuous form of the verbs in brackets. You may have to use some negative forms.

items.sentence: Gordon? I think he ...... (write) a letter at the moment.
Yes, the match is on TV now, but we ...... (lose).
Right now, Margaret ...... (have) a shower. Do you want to ring later?
Sally ...... (stay) with her aunt for a few days.
I ...... (lie)! It's true! I did see Madonna at the supermarket.
Josh ...... (always / use) my bike! It's so annoying.
We ...... (have) lunch, but I can come round and help you later.
...... (you / play) music up there? It's really noisy!

sections.title: C: Rewrite correctly
sections.instructions: Rewrite correctly. Change the words or phrases in bold.

items.sentence: **Are top musicians studying** for many years?
items.answer: Do top musicians study for many years?

items.sentence: What's going on? I hope you **don't touch** my things!
items.answer: What's going on? I hope you aren't touching my things!

items.sentence: It's a small business, so each person **is doing** lots of different jobs.
items.answer: It's a small business, so each person does lots of different jobs.

items.sentence: **Does Christine listen** to the radio, or is that the TV I can hear?
items.answer: Is Christine listening to the radio, or is that the TV I can hear?

items.sentence: I **am usually buying** a special ticket each week for the bus because it's cheaper.
items.answer: I usually buy a special ticket each week for the bus because it's cheaper.

items.sentence: Our washing machine **is starting** when you press this button.
items.answer: Our washing machine starts when you press this button.

items.sentence: How's the match going? **Does our team win**?
items.answer: How's the match going? Is our team winning?

items.sentence: Many people **are enjoying** spending time on the beach on holiday.
items.answer: Many people enjoy spending time on the beach on holiday.

sections.title: D: Circle the correct word or phrase
sections.instructions: Circle the correct word or phrase.

items.sentence: I **work / am working** at the local library for the summer.
We **don't go / aren't going** to the theatre very often.
Stacy **gets / is getting** ready for school, so she can't come to the phone.
**Does Gary ever talk / Is Gary ever talking** about his expedition to the Amazon jungle?
In squash, you **hit / are hitting** a ball against a wall.
I **read / am reading** a newspaper at least once a week.
**Do you practise / Are you practising** the piano for two hours every day?
Nadine and Claire **do / are doing** quite well at school at the moment.
A good friend **knows / is knowing** when you're upset about something.
How **do you spell / are you spelling** your name?

sections.title: E: Complete with present simple or present continuous
sections.instructions: Complete using the correct present simple or present continuous form of the verbs in the box. You may have to use some negative forms.

items.sentence: In Monopoly, you ...... around the board, buying houses and hotels.
...... you ...... this programme or can I turn the TV off?
Regular exercise ...... you to stay healthy.
I ...... my brother's guitar until I get a new one.
...... Simon always ...... the washing-up after lunch?
...... you ...... any sweaters in a larger size?
You ...... the kite right. Let me show you.
Dad ...... to the local astronomy club.

sections.title: F: Correct the wrong tenses
sections.instructions: Underline ten verbs in the wrong tense and rewrite them correctly.
sections.passage: 'One game I **am loving** (1) ...... is backgammon. You **are throwing** (2) ...... the dice and then you move your pieces around the board. It **is seeming** (3) ...... quite easy, but in fact you **are needing** (4) ...... to be quite careful. When your piece lands on one of the other person's pieces, you **are taking** (5) ...... it off the board and you send it back to the beginning. You **are winning** (6) ...... by getting all your pieces to the end and off the board. Some people **are preferring** (7) ...... chess, but I **am not understanding** (8) ...... that game. Right now, I **wait** (9) ...... to have a game with my brother. He **does** (10) ...... his homework. I usually win, so I think he doesn't want to play a game with me!'
